back_translation: The LORD said to Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt, saying,
'This month shall be for you the beginning of months; it shall be the first of the months of the year for you.'
'Speak to all the congregation of Israel, saying: On the tenth of this month each man shall take for himself a lamb according to the house of fathers, a lamb for the household.'
'If the household is too small for a lamb, he and his neighbor closest to his house shall take according to the number of persons; each according to his eating you shall count for the lamb.'
'A lamb without blemish, a male, son of a year, shall it be for you; from the sheep or from the goats you shall take.'
'It shall be for you for safekeeping until the fourteenth day of this month; all the assembly of the congregation of Israel shall slaughter it at twilight.'
'They shall take of the blood and put it on the two doorposts and on the lintel, on the houses where they eat it.'
'They shall eat the flesh that night, roasted with fire and unleavened bread; with bitter herbs they shall eat it.'
'Do not eat any of it raw or boiled in water, but only roasted with fire — its head with its legs and inner parts.'
'You shall not leave any of it until morning; what remains of it until morning you shall burn with fire.'
'And thus you shall eat it: your loins girded, your sandals on your feet, your staff in your hand; you shall eat it in haste — it is the Passover of the LORD.'
'I will pass through the land of Egypt that night and strike every firstborn in the land of Egypt, from man to beast; and against all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgments. I am the LORD.'
'The blood shall be for you a sign on the houses where you are; when I see the blood I will pass over you, and there shall be no plague among you to destroy when I strike in the land of Egypt.'
'This day shall be for you a memorial, and you shall celebrate it as a feast to the LORD throughout your generations — a permanent statute you shall celebrate it.'

'Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread; on the first day you shall remove leaven from your houses, for whoever eats anything leavened, that person shall be cut off from Israel — from the first day until the seventh day.'

'On the first day a sacred assembly, and on the seventh day a sacred assembly shall be for you; no work shall be done on them, except what is to be eaten by every person — that alone may be done by you.'
'You shall keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread, for on this very day I brought your divisions out of the land of Egypt; you shall keep this day throughout your generations as a permanent statute.'
'In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at evening, you shall eat unleavened bread, until the twenty-first day of the month at evening.'
'Seven days no leaven shall be found in your houses, for whoever eats what is leavened, that person shall be cut off from the congregation of Israel — whether a foreigner or native of the land.'
'You shall eat nothing leavened; in all your dwellings you shall eat unleavened bread.'
Moses called all the elders of Israel and said to them, 'Draw out and take for yourselves flocks for your families, and slaughter the Passover.'

'Take a cluster of hyssop, dip it in the blood that is in the basin, and touch the lintel and the two doorposts with the blood that is in the basin; none of you shall go out from the door of his house until morning.'

'For the LORD will pass through to strike the Egyptians; he will see the blood on the lintel and on the two doorposts, and the LORD will pass over the door and not allow the destroyer to come into your houses to strike.'
'You shall keep this thing as a statute for yourselves and for your sons forever.'
'When you come to the land that the LORD will give you as he has spoken, you shall keep this service.'
'When your children say to you, What does this service mean to you?'

'you shall say, It is the Passover sacrifice to the LORD, who passed over the houses of the sons of Israel in Egypt when he struck the Egyptians and delivered our houses.' Then the people bowed down and worshiped.
The sons of Israel went and did just as the LORD had commanded Moses and Aaron; so they did.

At midnight the LORD struck every firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sat on his throne to the firstborn of the captive who was in the dungeon, and every firstborn of cattle.
Pharaoh rose in the night — he and all his servants and all the Egyptians — and there was a great cry in Egypt, for there was not a house where there was no dead.
He called Moses and Aaron by night and said, 'Rise, go out from among my people, both you and the sons of Israel, and go, serve the LORD as you have spoken.'
'Take both your flocks and your herds, as you have said, and go; and bless me also.'
The Egyptians urged the people to send them out of the land in haste, for they said, 'We are all dying.'
The people took their dough before it was leavened; their kneading bowls bound up in their clothing on their shoulders.
The sons of Israel did according to the word of Moses; they asked from the Egyptians articles of silver, articles of gold, and clothing.
The LORD gave the people favor in the eyes of the Egyptians, and they granted their request; so they plundered the Egyptians.
The sons of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about 600,000 men on foot, besides little ones.
A great mixed multitude also went up with them, and flocks and herds — very heavy livestock.

They baked the dough they had brought out of Egypt as unleavened cakes, for it was not leavened, because they were driven out of Egypt and could not delay, nor had they prepared provisions for themselves.
The duration of the dwelling of the sons of Israel who dwelt in Egypt was 430 years.
At the end of 430 years, on that very day, all the divisions of the LORD went out from the land of Egypt.
It is a night of vigil for the LORD to bring them out from the land of Egypt; this same night is for the LORD a vigil for all the sons of Israel throughout their generations.
The LORD said to Moses and Aaron, 'This is the statute of the Passover: no son of a foreigner shall eat of it.'
'But every slave purchased with money, when you have circumcised him, then he may eat of it.'
'A sojourner and a hired hand shall not eat of it.'
'In one house it shall be eaten; you shall not bring out of the house any of the flesh outside, nor break a bone of it.'
'All the congregation of Israel shall do it.'

'When a foreigner sojourns with you and would keep the Passover to the LORD, let every male of his be circumcised, and then he may approach to keep it, and he shall be as a native of the land; but no uncircumcised man shall eat of it.'
'One law shall be for the native and for the foreigner who sojourns among you.'
All the sons of Israel did just as the LORD had commanded Moses and Aaron; so they did.
On that very day the LORD brought the sons of Israel out of the land of Egypt by their divisions.
